 168: 
 169: 
 170:     """
 171:     def set_which_diffs(self, int[::1] which_diffs):
 172:         self.d_di = which_diffs
 173:         self.n_d_di = self.d_di.shape[0]
 174: 
 175:     def get_which_diffs(self):
 176:         return np.asarray(self.d_di), self.n_d_di
 177: 
 178:     cdef dexpmH_pointer(self, double[::1] c, double complex *u0, double complex *du0, dt=1.0):  #int[::1] d_di,
 179:         # Construct Hamiltonian
 180:         cdef int i
 181:         cdef double complex *h0
 182:         cdef int curr_d_di_1
 183:         self.weighted_hamiltonian(c)
 184:         zheevd(self.jobz, self.uplo, &self.d, self.v0, &self.d, self.e0, self.work0, &self.lwork, self.rwork0, &self.lrwork, self.iwork0, &self.liwork, &self.info)
 185:         copy_pointer(self.v0, self.u1, self.d2)
 186:         v_exp_pointer(self.e0, self.u1, dt, self.d)
 187:         DagM_M_cdot_pointer(self.v0, self.u1, u0, self.d)
 188:         # Calculate the differentials
 189:         for i in range(self.n_d_di): # According to https://arxiv.org/pdf/2006.00935.pdf (Daalgard, Motzoi paper --> Original source?)
 190:             curr_d_di_1 = self.d_di[i] + 1
 191:             h0 = &self.H[curr_d_di_1, 0, 0]
 192:             MM_cdot_scale_pointer(dt, self.v0, h0, self.u1, self.d) #self.u1, self.d)
 193:             M_DagM_cdot_pointer(self.u1, self.v0, du0, self.d)
 194: 
 195:             phase_shift_matrix_pointer(self.e0, self.c1, self.u1, dt, self.d)
 196:             AxB_elementwise_pointer(du0, self.u1, self.u2, self.d2)
 197: 
 198:             MM_cdot_pointer( self.u2, self.v0, self.u1, self.d)
 199:             DagM_M_cdot_pointer(self.v0, self.u1, du0, self.d)
 200:             du0 += self.d2
 201:     def dexpmH(self, double[::1] c, double complex[:,::1] U, double complex[:,:,::1] dU, double dt=1.0):  #int[::1] d_di,
 202:         # d_di contains the indexes of the derivatives that we want to calculate (needs to be in ascending order with a negative value at the end)
 203:         cdef double complex *u0 = &U[0, 0]
 204:         cdef double complex *du0 = &dU[0,0,0]
 205:         if not c.shape[0] == self.n_dims_1:
 206:             raise ValueError('The coefficient c must be of size [interpolation_dimensions].')
 207:         if not self.d_di.shape[0] == dU.shape[0]:
 208:             raise ValueError('Inputs must fulfill: which_diffs.shape[0] = dU.shape[0].')
 209:         self.dexpmH_pointer(c, u0, du0, dt)
 210: 
 211:     cdef expmH_pulse_pointer(self, double[:,::1] cs, double complex *u0, double dt=1.0):
 212:         cdef int i
 213:         cdef int steps = cs.shape[0]
 214: 
 215:         self.weighted_hamiltonian(cs[0,:])
 216:         zheevd(self.jobz, self.uplo, &self.d, self.v0, &self.d, self.e0, self.work0, &self.lwork, self.rwork0, &self.lrwork, self.iwork0, &self.liwork, &self.info)
 217:         copy_pointer(self.v0, self.u1, self.d2)
 218:         v_exp_pointer(self.e0, self.v0, dt, self.d)
 219:         DagM_M_cdot_pointer(self.u1, self.v0, u0, self.d)
 220: 
 221:         for i in range(1,steps):
 222:             self.weighted_hamiltonian(cs[i, :])
 223:             zheevd(self.jobz, self.uplo, &self.d, self.v0, &self.d, self.e0, self.work0, &self.lwork, self.rwork0, &self.lrwork, self.iwork0, &self.liwork, &self.info)
 224:             MM_cdot_pointer(self.v0, u0, self.u1, self.d)
 225:             v_exp_pointer(self.e0, self.u1, dt, self.d)
 226:             DagM_M_cdot_pointer(self.v0, self.u1, u0, self.d)
 227:     def expmH_pulse(self, double[:,::1] cs, double complex[:,::1] U, double dt=1.0):
 228:         # Construct Hamiltonian
 229:         cdef double complex *u0 = &U[0, 0]
 230:         self.expmH_pulse_pointer(cs, u0, dt)
 231: 
 232:     def grape(self, double[:,::1] cs, double complex[:,::1] U_target, int[::1] target_indexes, double complex[:,::1] U, double complex[:,:,::1] dU, double[:,::1] dI_dj):
 233:         # Calculate fidelity for a pulse and the differentials of the fidelity at every timestep using the grape trick
 234:         cdef int i, j
 235:         cdef int steps = cs.shape[0]
 236: 
 237:         cdef double complex *new_p0 = self.u1
 238:         cdef double complex *new_q0 = self.u2
 239:         cdef double complex *p0 = self.u4
 240:         cdef double complex *q0 = self.u5
 241:         cdef double complex *u0 = &U[0, 0]
 242:         cdef double complex *u_tar = &U_target[0, 0]
 243:         cdef double complex *curr_u = self.u3
 244:         cdef double complex *du = &dU[0,0,0]
 245: 
 246:         cdef double complex trM, trdM, two_nni
 247:         cdef double n = <double> target_indexes.shape[0]
 248:         cdef double nni = 1.0 / (n * (n + 1))
 249:         cdef double one_minus = 1.0 - n * nni
 250: 
 251:         if not dI_dj.shape[0] == steps:
 252:             raise ValueError('Inputs must fulfill: cs.shape[0] = dI_dj.shape[0].')
 253:         if not dI_dj.shape[1] == self.n_d_di:
 254:             raise ValueError('Inputs must fulfill: which_diffs.shape[0] = dI_dj.shape[1].')
 255:         if not cs.shape[1] == self.n_d_di:
 256:             raise ValueError('Inputs must fulfill: which_diffs.shape[0] = cs.shape[1].')
 257:         # Fidelity constants
 258: 
 259:         self.expmH_pulse_pointer(cs, u0)
 260:         #Dag_fast(U_target, self.Ur3) # -> Ur3 is identical to Q  -> replaced by new approach
 261:         DagM_M_cdot_pointer(u_tar, u0, q0, self.d)
 262:         trM = target_indexes_trace_pointer(q0, self.d, target_indexes)
 263:         two_nni = - 2 / (n * (n + 1)) * conj(trM)
 264:         cdef double I0 = one_minus - nni * abs_2(trM) # Sub Fidelity
 265: 
 266:         # Do GRAPE trick
 267:         self.dexpmH_pointer(cs[0,:], curr_u, du) # Calculate Unitary and its derivatives
 268:         M_DagM_cdot_pointer(q0, curr_u, new_q0, self.d)  # Reduce q0
 269:         copy_pointer(new_q0, q0, self.d2)
 270:         for j in range(self.n_d_di):
 271:             #MM_cdot_pointer(curr_du, p0, new_p0, self.d)  # p0 is not defined yet
 272:             trdM = tr_dot_pointer_target_indexes(q0, du, self.d, target_indexes)
 273:             dI_dj[0, j] = ( two_nni * trdM ).real
 274:             du += self.d2
 275:         copy_pointer(curr_u, p0, self.d2)  # Define p0 from curr_u
 276:         for i in range(1, steps):
 277:             du -= self.d2 * self.n_d_di
 278:             self.dexpmH_pointer(cs[i, :], curr_u, du)  # Calculate Unitary and its derivatives
 279:             M_DagM_cdot_pointer(q0, curr_u, new_q0, self.d)  # Remove current unitary (curr_u) from Q
 280:             for j in range(self.n_d_di):
 281:                 MM_cdot_pointer(du, p0, new_p0, self.d)
 282:                 trdM = tr_dot_pointer_target_indexes(new_q0, new_p0, self.d, target_indexes)
 283:                 dI_dj[i, j] = ( two_nni * trdM ).real
 284:                 du += self.d2
 285:             MM_cdot_pointer(curr_u, p0, new_p0, self.d)  # Add current unitary (curr_u) to P
 286:             # flip pointers
 287:             self.u1, self.u4 = self.u4, self.u1
 288:             self.u2, self.u5 = self.u5, self.u2
 289:             new_p0, p0 = p0, new_p0
 290:             new_q0, q0 = q0, new_q0
 291:         return I0
 292:     """
